context.reason: The EP8 holistic profile ranks combine_dx peer-arrival skew ahead of attention, loss, copies, and RCCL for the current guarded systems canary.

code_changes: Added an opt-in staged AsyncLL dX return path behind TORCHTITAN_MORI_AITER_DX_RETURN_MODE=asyncll_staged.
Made x, grad, and top-k dummy dispatch use the same AsyncLL route family under staged mode.
Changed dTopk combine to use AsyncLL combine_send/combine_recv when topk_op is AsyncLL.
Added an AsyncLL max_total_recv_tokens default of max_tokens * top_k * 2 to avoid long-sequence receive overflow while staying below all-to-one worst-case buffers.

runs.read: Strict source-position guard passes at S128 after making the top-k return route AsyncLL-consistent.

runs.failure: AsyncLL device assert: totalTokens <= config.MaxNumTokensToRecv(); increase maxTotalRecvTokens.
runs.read: The S4096 path reached AsyncLL dispatch/receive and failed on capacity, not on the earlier route-family mismatch.

runs.failure: grad_output MORI source-position set mismatch after the AsyncLL receive budget fix.
runs.read: The capacity fix clears the receive-token assert; the remaining strict-mode blocker is AsyncLL source-position metadata/order validation at long sequence.
Long-sequence staged AsyncLL can complete a full TorchTitan backward only when the source-position guard is bypassed. This is a route-contract diagnostic, not a retained performance setting.

what_is_confirmed: Top-k route consistency is fixed for staged AsyncLL.
AsyncLL receive capacity must be set explicitly or derived for S4096 routed rows.
The S4096 data path can complete with assumed dispatch order, so the remaining immediate blocker is source-position metadata/order validation rather than the staged dX compute split itself.
what_is_not_confirmed: No retained throughput win is proven.
The assume-order run is not a correctness proof; it needs parity against the strict sync/intranode path or a trustworthy AsyncLL source-position mapping.
CUDA-event timing sidecars synchronize profiled regions and should not be used as a no-profile throughput baseline.

next_actions: Build a deterministic parity probe for AsyncLL staged dX/dTopk versus the retained sync route, using the same top-k tensor and checking dX, dTopk, and local expert gradients.
Inspect or replace AsyncLL get_dispatch_src_token_pos handling for long S4096 routes; if metadata is unreliable, derive ordering from a shared route-state map rather than assuming it.
Only after parity passes, run no-profile 4x S4096 GBS8/GBS64 A/B against retained sync combine, then promote to EP8.
